title: The MACU Report — Episode 18 — "Nobody Leaves the Chair"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip (animate), do NOT first-frame. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

fields.sub: A FEAST. A LIST. A LOCK.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

earl.core: a hopeful wary ordinary man in a patched coat, nervous hopeful smile

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, lounging on a reclined dental chair like a throne, big grin

gourmand.core: a sleek sinister chef in a dark apron at a kitchen counter, holding one long knife, thin cold smile

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed in front of a chained doorway, gold chain, theatrical posing

rose.core: a sweet elderly woman with soft white hair in a floral apron and pearls, beaming, stirring an enormous pot in a ruined concrete bunker kitchen

seth.core: a nervous sweating gaunt man in a frayed coat, gap-toothed, glancing fearfully to the side, dim

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. Day three, and the community has never been bigger. Three hundred and forty souls, all in one place, all on my list.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Three hundred and forty, Ron. You've been at three forty since lunch. The record is four hundred. The biggest gathering in MACU history. You're so close. Don't you want to be the man who did that?

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Four hundred. I can get four hundred.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Or you could stop at a pleasant three hundred and forty and go home, Ron.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Some of them would also like to go home, Ron.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Pleasant doesn't make history, Walter. Pleasant doesn't bring a whole town home. To the hall!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I was up all night on this, MACU. Here's the play: effective immediately, I'm consolidating. The community drive and Chef Rose's feast are now ONE operation. The feast hall is headquarters. One roof, one list. Rose — here. Three days, every door, every name, in my own hand. Take it. They're our people now.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Oh, Ron. The whole list? Every single name? You've made an old cook so happy. Bring them all to the table. Every last one. There's always room. I've never had a full house before. I'm going to need a bigger pot.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: You've given the cook a list of everyone you could find and locked them in her hall. Very organized, Ron.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: A real operation needs a door. Though — chaining folks in, at a feast, I don't know if that's

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: It's protection, Ron. You know what it's like when people scatter. You don't want to lose everyone again.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...No. No, I don't. Dr. Goldtooth lent me his man. Roscoe — front of house. Lock it down. Keep our people safe inside where I can look after them.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: These doors are CLOSED, brother. You try the handle — the handle tries YOU. The last fella who reached for an exit on Roscoe's watch? He is now PART of the WALL. Say hi on your way to your seat — THAT'S him, that's the load-bearing one. He holds up the EAST SIDE now. He found a PURPOSE. Snap into your chair. OHHH yeahhh.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Same security Dr. Goldtooth trusts with his gold. That's how serious we are about keeping everybody together.

cues.speaker: EARL
cues.vo: ...Jesus. ...Cozy, though! Honestly — why would I ever leave? Best week of my life, Ron. I'm exactly where I'm supposed to be.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: With the doors secured, it's time for Scavenger's Feast — and today's celebrity guest chef, a man of exquisite taste.

cues.speaker: THE GOURMAND
cues.vo: Thank you, Walter. People think you want them afraid first. The fear tenderizes nothing — it's a myth. No. You want them HAPPY. Grateful. Gathered willingly. A man who walks in on his own two feet is the finest cut there is.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Today's centerpiece came to us so willingly. A Mr. Dwyer. Volunteered for the community. We're doing him as a roast — Dwyer was always warm.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Oh, Dwyer. That's — Walter, you can TASTE how willing he was. THIS is what generosity tastes like, MACU. Give of yourself. Look what we make together.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: He really did give of himself. Right down to the dish. Mr. Dwyer had a very good attitude about things, right to the end. We'll miss his column.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Doors LOCKED? Baby, that is PERFECT for business — nobody's goin' anywhere, so let's talk VOLUME. Bulk rate on molars, today only. Come see Dr. Goldtooth and leave a little lighter. Lighter than you think. Doc's got a gift for the guest of honor, too. Something personal. Something you don't walk away from with. Free of charge.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Three-sixty. Three-seventy. Walter, we're going to break it.

cues.speaker: SETH
cues.vo: Ron. Ron, listen — I counted. Three days, three hundred people, and you know what's NOT arrived? Food. No crops. No cans. Nothing's coming IN. The only thing showing up is people. Ron — the feast isn't FOR us. The feast IS

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I KNOW. I know something's wrong out there, friend — I've known for two years. That's exactly why we're all in here TOGETHER, where it's safe. You're one of us now. Sign in. Stick close. Don't wander. You're protected here.

cues.speaker: SETH
cues.vo: ...They didn't even ask anybody.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Step count: six hundred and twelve, Ron. Busy day!

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: A heavy thought from Seth there. Anyway — the weather.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Your forecast: the haze has lifted entirely, a warmth on the wind, condition rating exceptionally mild. Lovely weather to stay indoors. With the doors locked.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. As of tonight the community and the feast are one. Three hundred and ninety souls, one roof, doors secured. Tomorrow we hit four hundred — a record — and not one person left behind. We have never been safer. We have never been closer.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Three hundred and ninety, Ron. Ten to go. You're going to break the record. ...One more day.
